profile.name: Santiago Suaznabar
profile.title: Full-Stack Developer & AI Engineer
profile.bio: I build production AI systems - from retrieval pipelines and agents to the interfaces that make them legible. This portfolio is a live demo of that work.

modules.label: RAG
modules.description: A retrieval-augmented generation pipeline, using Redis as the semantic cache, Qdrant as the vector store with a Hybrid Search as a retrieval strategy(One dense vector and one sparse vector), applied a Query Reformulator before making the search.

topic.title: Three Days of Happiness
topic.author: Sugaru Miaki
topic.synopsis: Three Days of Happiness (also known as I Sold My Life for 10,000 Yen a Year) follows Kusunoki, a broke, 20-year-old with no future prospects. He discovers a shop that buys lifespans, where he learns his remaining 30 years are valued at a mere ¥300,000 (about $2,000). He sells all but his final three months to enjoy the money. Kusunoki, jaded and believing his life is worthless, accepts the abysmal trade-off. However, the lifespan shop has a strict policy: to prevent impulsive or reckless behavior from those who have sold their time, the company assigns an 'observer' to monitor the client until their final days. Enter Miyagi, a quiet, emotionless young woman who becomes Kusunoki's constant companion during the final quarter of his life.

sample questions: What is the name of the main character?
How much money was his lifespan worth?
Why did Himeno reach out again?
Why Miyagi ended up as an observer?
What does Miyagi and Kusunoki do in the end?

wakeup.message: Backend runs on Render's free tier and spins down after ~15 min of inactivity.

notices.label: Cold Start
notices.detail: Render free tier spins down after ~15 min of inactivity. First load can take up to 60s.

notices.label: Rate Limit
notices.detail: 10 requests per minute per session to protect the shared API key.

notices.label: Gemini API
notices.detail: Responses use a personal free-tier Gemini key. Heavy usage may hit daily token limits.

notices.label: Topic
notices.detail: The Vector Store was fed with the book 'Three Days of Happiness'
